title: Writing - Part 1: Immigration and Cultural Exchange
description: Your class has been discussing the impact of immigration on society.

content.question: Write an essay discussing two of the impacts in your notes. You should explain which impact you consider most significant, giving reasons for your opinion.

opinions: Immigration makes a country more diverse and interesting.
Large numbers of newcomers can put strain on housing and healthcare.
Immigrants often do jobs that local workers are unwilling to take.

structure.introduction: Introduce the topic of immigration and the two impacts you will discuss.
structure.bodyParagraph1: Discuss the first impact (e.g., cultural diversity) and how it enriches society.
structure.bodyParagraph2: Discuss the second impact (e.g., economic contributions) and its importance.
structure.conclusion: Evaluate both impacts and state which you consider most significant.

content.modelAnswer:
Immigration has always shaped the history and identity of nations, and it continues to be one of the most debated topics in politics and society today. While concerns exist, immigration also brings considerable benefits. This essay will discuss how immigration enriches culture and how immigrants contribute economically to their adopted countries.

One of the most valuable effects of immigration is the cultural diversity it brings. Immigrant communities introduce new languages, cuisines, traditions and perspectives that enrich the social and cultural life of a country. Cities with diverse populations tend to be more creative, innovative and open-minded, as exposure to different cultures broadens everyone's worldview.

The economic contributions of immigrants are equally significant. Many immigrants fill vital roles in industries facing labour shortages, from agriculture and construction to medicine and technology. They pay taxes, start businesses and create jobs, making a substantial contribution to the prosperity of their new home. Without immigration, many economies would struggle to maintain their current levels of growth.

In conclusion, while cultural diversity is a wonderful benefit of immigration, I believe the economic contribution is the most significant impact. In an era of ageing populations and labour shortages, immigration plays a crucial role in sustaining economic activity and funding public services such as healthcare and pensions. This practical reality is perhaps the strongest argument for welcoming immigration with open and thoughtful policies.
